skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through structured code reviews for bugs, security issues, performance concerns, and maintainability feedback.
skill.summary: Run structured code reviews with prioritized findings and practical fixes.
skill.author: 21pounder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and three weak cryptography patterns, but all are false positives in SKILL.md. The command-like text is a fenced JSON schema example, and the weak crypto hits are ordinary descriptive text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network behavior, or executable script behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 14 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. This line starts a fenced Markdown JSON block and is not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown code fence for a JSON example. No shell, Ruby, or command execution context exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Flagged as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line is frontmatter describing when to use the skill and contains no cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text for a code review skill. It does not define or invoke any cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Parameter Description Flagged as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 20 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line describes a target path parameter inside a JSON schema example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 20 is plain schema documentation for a file path, directory, or glob target. No crypto function or implementation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Focus Description Flagged as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 25 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line describes a focus field in a JSON schema example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 25 is a natural-language parameter description. It has no executable cryptographic behavior.

content.user_title: Review Code for Bugs and Security Issues
content.value_statement: Code changes often hide bugs, security gaps, and maintainability problems. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured reviews with prioritized feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews files, directories, or glob targets supplied by the user.
Checks logic, security, performance, style, and maintainability concerns.
Prioritizes findings by severity from critical to low.
Provides line references and explains why each issue matters.
Suggests concrete fixes and code examples when useful.
Can produce review output in the user's requested language.
limitations: It only reads files and searches project content through allowed tools.
It does not run tests, builds, linters, or security scanners by itself.
It depends on the files and context available in the current workspace.
It may miss runtime issues that require executing the application.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Find bugs, risky changes, and missing tests before a pull request is merged.

use_cases.title: Check Security-Sensitive Code
use_cases.description: Inspect authentication, input handling, and data access code for common vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Improve Maintainability
use_cases.description: Identify confusing structure, duplicated logic, and inconsistent style in a module.

prompt_templates.title: Basic File Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the file at [path] for bugs and maintainability issues. Prioritize findings by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Security Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review [path] with a security focus. Check input validation, authorization, data exposure, and unsafe dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Directory Quality Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a module or feature directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the directory [path] for logic errors, duplicated patterns, missing tests, and unclear design choices.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review [target] for release readiness. Include critical risks, regression risks, test gaps, and recommended fixes.

output: High: Password reset tokens may remain valid after use. Invalidate tokens immediately after successful reset.
Medium: Login error messages reveal whether an account exists. Use a generic response for all failures.
Test gap: Add tests for expired tokens, reused tokens, and locked accounts.
Medium: Date parsing logic is duplicated across three helpers. Extract a shared parser with documented timezone behavior.
Low: Several helper names hide side effects. Rename them or split mutation from formatting.
Positive: Error handling is consistent across the reviewed utility functions.

best_practices: Provide the exact files or directories that should be reviewed.
State the review focus, such as security, performance, or maintainability.
Include related tests or documentation so feedback considers project intent.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a broad repository review without narrowing the scope.
Do not treat review output as a replacement for tests or scanners.
Do not ignore severity ordering when planning fixes.

faq.question: Can this skill review security issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes security review steps for injection, authorization, validation, and data exposure risks.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for reading and searching files, not running builds or tests.

faq.question: Can it review an entire directory?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports file paths, directories, and glob patterns when those files are available.

faq.question: Will it provide line numbers?
faq.answer: Yes. The methodology asks for specific line references when reporting issues.

faq.question: Can it respond in another language?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include multilingual review output when the user requests it.

faq.question: Is this a substitute for automated testing?
faq.answer: No. It complements tests and scanners by adding contextual review and prioritized human-readable feedback.
